item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While articles don't need a backstory and four thousand logs to be complete, there does need to be some there there. This just seems like the hook was "what if good thing was… bad." I don't care for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really just a thing what kills you, when you get down to it. There's no plot beyond a somewhat unique methodology, and it tops the cake off with a compulsion, so there's no actual tension of any kind. Further, there's some absurdly strong idiot ball with the implication that people can't connect the shirt they're wearing saying "free hugs" with the fact that people suddenly just have to get a hug from them.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image could not be found under any source as cc-compliant, and the second image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I start reading this thinking "Oh, this seems harmless enough, just compels people to hug? Pffft."
And then I read the crushing part…
I'm pretty sure I said "Oh Jesus Christ" at several points. Definite +1 here
giggles

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whether this is a memetic effect caused by unique visual properties of the text or a psychic compulsion caused by agents unknown is under investigation

I know some ways that they can test this:

  • If it's memetic, then theoretically, not being able to see it should stop it.
  • If it's psychic, then Telekill Alloy should be able to block it

Try those tests (unless as an author you want it to remain a mystery).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good question! The implication I tried to make is that testing with this item is ongoing. They don't even know if it's a memetic effect, something to do with the unique way it's written… Or some kind of magical/reality-altering property of the item itself. Testing with illiterate or non-English speakers would definitely be something they'd look at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does the reader have to be able to read English for the effect to take, or will it affect any human seeing it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Srsly agreed. Amazing read, great/short/simple concept. Straightforward imagination all up in this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed reading this SCP. It is both well written and is deceivingly innocent. Here, have a free HUG upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See? It just goes to show there's no such thing as a free hug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank goodness it didn't say "Make Love, Not War" !

…or, is one of those out there, too?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They know EXACTLY what's going on. And they don't like it (unless they're a psychopath) especially since the arms are making their motions all on their own, and they're involuntarily crushing people to death who are just giving them happy, sympathetic smiles and trying to make *them* feel better.

And that disturbing image is exactly why it got a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is the best use of the NERRRRDS scream I've ever seen. Bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never thought someone could make a deadly artefact out of a free hugs shirt…
Seems I was wrong. Upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't be so cranky. You just need a hug!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw both pictures, checked to make sure the writing wasn't crap, and upvoted the hell out of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr. Bright upvoted! My life has meaning again. <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Boring effect, but the mental image of someone wearing this shirt, running frantically from a crowd of people who want to hug them, was entertaining, and a bit disturbing. So upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… which con were you thinking of, Bright?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Over The Top… uh, shirt?
+0
I would've liked it more if the wearer wasn't confunded while the groping was going on and was all-too-aware of what was happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

I want my free hug now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was getting ready to downvote… and then it made me smile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but such short bursts that you could only crush two or three people before your muscles give out. We're built for endurance, not strength.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delightful, cheerful evil. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Compulsive thing what kills you. There's nothing here for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, that isn't super-strength. The human body is capable of far greater degrees of physical exertion than we typically put it through, for short periods. Most people have a subconscious internal censor that prevents us from using our muscles to max because we would injure ourselves by overstraining the muscles, connective tissue or even the bones themselves. It's why people who are in immediate-emergency adrenaline rush mode can do things like lift cars single-handedly long enough for someone trapped underneath to crawl out.

And apparently whatever is controlling the arms to hug doesn't really give a crap about your mental limits on hug strength.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't usually enjoy things what kill people, but there is something very evil in the idea of killer hugs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it doesn't grab ya, it doesn't grab ya, I guess! Thanks for reading. Incidentally, they don't KNOW that it's a memetic effect… In my thinking, it was created to cause slaughter in crowded areas where personal space is disregarded, like a comic convention.

Like if this guy…

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IRuuF-5ZST8

… was a wizard. He made it to kill nerds!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The auto-tag did that for me, I think, I confess I forgot all about tagging. I am dead tired and need sleep right now, but I will review and edit the tags soon, promise!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not useful super-strength, at least; just the power to traumatically kill someone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I'm not really a fan. The effect is kind of obvious, and there's no real hook. It just kills people. Plus, it strikes me as off that what is apparently a memetic effect also grants the wearer super-strength.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm surprised this works out, though I suspect some people will complain it should be a -J.

Personally, I think it could be mainlist since it vilifies an existing innocent phenomenon and could creep out people familiar with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The terminology is a bit off. Check out the Tag Guide for the difference between a compulsion and a mind-affecting effect, a compulsion is by definition resistible and you specifically state that the affected individuals cannot resist the urge to hug the person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I had lots of help from some lovely people in the chat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Written well enough to be taken seriously, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've seen people at conventions lined up to heed the siren song of the "free hugs" shirt, even when the wearer is flabby, acne-scarred, and of questionable personal hygiene. There's something sinister in its allure…
